page_content: PHYS662 Intersections of Technology and Policy: Modernizing the Energy System (3 Credits) A broad, practical introduction to the issues and assessment approaches used to evaluate technical innovation in the Energy System. Introduction to the use of Sankey Diagrams, Life Cycle Analysis, Techno-economic analysis, and Equilibrium Economic Analysis, as well as policy factors such as Energy Efﬁciency Standards, Vehicle Fuel Economy, Feed-in- tariffs and environmental regulations. Restriction: Must be a graduate student in the Natural Sciences, Engineering, Public Policy, or Economics; or must be a senior in the above disciplines with a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0. Credit Only Granted for: PHYS662 or PLCY699B.
